functional_requirements.title: In-memory user credential store with hashed passwords
functional_requirements.description: The system maintains a simulated user database in memory that maps usernames to their password hashes using SHA-256.
acceptance_criteria: A dictionary exists mapping usernames (e.g., 'admin', 'user') to their SHA-256 password hashes.
New authentication logic relies on comparing hashed input passwords to stored hashes.

functional_requirements.title: Password hashing function
functional_requirements.description: Provide a function hash_password(password) that returns the SHA-256 hex digest of the given password.
acceptance_criteria: Given any password string, hash_password returns the correct SHA-256 hex digest.
The function is used consistently for both input password processing and comparison against stored hashes.

functional_requirements.title: User authentication
functional_requirements.description: Authenticate a user by verifying that the provided username exists and the SHA-256 hash of the provided password matches the stored hash.
acceptance_criteria: authenticate with an unknown username or incorrect password returns False.

functional_requirements.title: CLI login flow with French prompts
functional_requirements.description: Provide a command-line login flow that prompts for username and password using French labels and prints appropriate success or failure messages.
acceptance_criteria: Prompts shown are 'Nom d'utilisateur: ' and 'Mot de passe: '.
On successful authentication, prints ' Connexion réussie !'.
On failed authentication, prints ' Identifiants incorrects'.

functional_requirements.title: Script entry point to trigger login
functional_requirements.description: When the module is executed as the main program, it should initiate the login flow automatically.
acceptance_criteria: Running the file directly (not imported) starts the login process via login().
No login is triggered when the module is imported by another module.

functional_requirements.title: UI localization and messaging
functional_requirements.description: User prompts and messages are localized to French and are exactly as defined in the code.
acceptance_criteria: Prompts and messages match the French text used in the code.
No additional localization logic is introduced beyond the existing strings.

functional_requirements.title: Security considerations (not production-ready)
functional_requirements.description: This implementation uses SHA-256 without a salt and stores credentials in memory; it is not suitable for production use without enhancements (e.g., salting, peppering, persistent secure storage, rate limiting).
acceptance_criteria: Code explicitly relies on unsalted SHA-256 hashes stored in memory.
No rate limiting, no account lockout, and no persistent credential store are implemented.

user_stories.title: User login with valid credentials
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to log in using a username and password so that I can access the system.
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid username and password, when I attempt to log in, then authentication succeeds and the system prints ' Connexion réussie !'.

user_stories.title: Password hashing is used for authentication
user_stories.description: As a developer, I want the system to hash passwords with SHA-256 before comparing, so that plaintext passwords are not stored or compared directly.
acceptance_criteria: Given any password, hash_password(password) produces a SHA-256 hex digest that is used for comparison against stored hashes.

user_stories.title: French CLI prompts and feedback
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to see login prompts and results in French, aligning with the UI language of the application.
acceptance_criteria: Prompts and messages shown match the strings in the code: 'Nom d'utilisateur: ', 'Mot de passe: ', '=== AUTHENTIFICATION ===', ' Connexion réussie !', ' Identifiants incorrects'.

business_rules.rule: Passwords are stored as SHA-256 hashes without salts in an in-memory store.
business_rules.implementation: In code, the users_db maps usernames to hashlib.sha256(password).hexdigest(). The authentication path hashes the input password and compares to the stored value.
business_rules.validation: Authentication succeeds only when the input password hash equals the corresponding stored hash; otherwise it fails.

business_rules.rule: This implementation is not production-ready due to absence of salt, rate limiting, and persistent secure storage.
business_rules.implementation: Commentary and REQ-007 indicate the lack of salting and other hardening measures.
business_rules.validation: Security reviews should flag the absence of salt and anti-br brute-force measures; any production deployment should address these concerns.

data_flows.transformation: Collects username and password strings from prompts
data_flows.validation: Prompts are displayed; inputs are captured as strings
Produces a hex digest representing the hashed password

data_flows.transformation: Lookup of stored hashed password by username
data_flows.validation: Stored hash is retrieved if username exists

data_flows.transformation: Equality comparison between input hash and stored hash
data_flows.validation: Returns True if hashes match; otherwise False
If True, prints success message; if False, prints failure message
